widgets_values:
CASTLE DMP — MARKETING BUILD

Sea-cliff castle: sky · ocean · castle+rocks.

Queue 1: the 🧭 VLM reads the plate and fills the SAM3 prompts
below (sam_sky / sam_fg rails). auto_continue is ON, so the same
queue runs the solve and stops at the ✅ gate.

Read the 🧭 report, then ✅ Approve Solve and queue again.

Needs lmstudio on :1234 with a VISION model. No VLM? Set
auto_continue ON and type the prompts into the SAM3 nodes by hand
— everything downstream still works.

SCALE — READ THIS

height_mode=assume, camera_height_m=40.

Single-image scale is ambiguous. On an elevated vista the default
1.6 m eye height is ~10x too small, and EVERY metric downstream
follows it — 📏 band cutoffs, the hole-fill band box, DCC cameras.

40 m is a sighting-in guess for a clifftop camera. Dial it until
the ℹ Info HUD's scene depth looks right, or swap in
AtlasScaleOverride / a reference-scale node.

Nothing here is 'wrong' at 1.6 — it is just 25x small.

WHY TWO DEPTH MODELS

MoGe is the near-field specialist but its far field runs away
(>1000 m) and it CULLS SKY — disqualifying for a sky dome.
DA3/V2-Outdoor behave in the far field.

So: MoGe -> castle+rocks. DA3 -> sky dome + water.
The depth model is a PER-BAND choice; a node default is just the
starting value of a widget you override per band anyway.

DA3 needs the [neural-da3] extra (--no-deps, see INSTALL.md).
castle and cliff and rocks and grass
nodes.title: SAM3 · castle + rocks (LITERAL)

widgets_values:
SAM3 PROMPTS — measured on THIS plate (coverage of the castle band vs the bottom rocks):
  'medieval castle and rocky cliff'             castle 44.1%  bottom  6.2%   <- loses the foreground rocks
  'medieval castle, rocky cliff, grass, rocks'  castle  0.0%  bottom  0.0%   <- COMMAS = EMPTY MASK, silently
  'castle and cliff and rocks and grass'        castle 44.3%  bottom 70.8%   <- SHIPPED: castle AND the rocks

Join terms with AND. A comma-separated list is the obvious way to ask for several objects, and it no-matches to
ZERO with no error — which then INVERTS into an exclude-EVERYTHING mask and empties the layer to zero mesh.

SKY DOME

distance_m=1200 puts the card far back (parallax); radius_m=800 is
its MINIMUM half-extent (size), grown by outpaint — distance and
size are decoupled.

edge_extend_px smears sky colour past the silhouette (deterministic
Nuke-style edge-extend, NOT an inpaint). frame_outpaint_px=96 pads
past the FRAME so small orbits never hit the plate boundary.

LaMa's seed is PINNED to 0 — ComfyUI auto-adds 'randomize' to any
widget named seed, which silently re-rolls the plate every queue.

ONE SPLIT, BOTH LAYERS

W = P95-P5 of the castle's depth over its matte.
cutoff = near + 2·W  ->  'the relief may extrude back at most twice
its own width'.

The SAME band_split feeds both layers:
  castle  band_side=foreground -> [0, cutoff]   (relief clipped)
  water   band_side=background -> [cutoff, inf] (pushed back)

Because the split is an ABSOLUTE distance (not a percentile over
each layer's own pixels), both resolve the identical boundary —
no band drift, no band_ref_mask needed.

SEAM DOCTRINE — the smear lives on the layers BEHIND.
The FRONTMOST band keeps a CLEAN cut matte (edge_extend_px=0); every band behind it gets edge_extend.
Priorities are FARTHEST-HIGHEST (sky -10 < water -5 < castle 0) so at a watertight seam the layer BEHIND wins
the depth near-tie — nearest-highest makes each band's smear render IN FRONT of the layer behind it (striped seams).

WHY A SEPARATE BRANCH

AtlasExportReliefMesh reads projection_scene.proxy_geometry —
which AtlasDeriveReliefMesh writes. Clean-plate layers append to
projection_sources instead, so a castle built as a LAYER is not
exportable by this node at all.

So: the DMP scene (group 8) and the exportable mesh (here) are two
products off the same gated solve. exclude_mask = NOT(castle) is
what cuts the ocean and sky out of the OBJ.

🔧 HOLE FILL — export-only, and invisible in the MASTER viewport by design.
A tear IS a depth discontinuity; filling one in the live mesh would bridge exactly what the tearing prevents.
So read the report ON the node, and wire preview_solve into THIS viewport to see what actually ships.

max_hole_edges counts GRID edges — it scales with relief_grid. 128 @ grid 1024 ≈ 64 @ grid 512.
Measured on this plate: 128 -> 97 holes/+1316 faces; 1024 -> 101 holes/+2669 faces. The 4 extra are 128-617 edge
loops = big flat caps over real silhouettes. 128 buys 97 of 101 holes for HALF the geometry.

Band box (fill_depth_near_m/far_m) is OFF: both must be > 0, it needs a TRUSTWORTHY metric scale, and window mode
bypasses the largest-loop guard (the one way to accidentally cap the outer frame). Set the camera height first.

groups.title: 0 · PLATE + 🧭 VLM PRE-FLIGHT — Queue 1 costs only the assessment; auto_continue lets it flow straight on to the ✅ solve gate
1 · SOLVE + ✅ GATE — a cheap solve first; approve it before paying for depth/SAM/inpaint
2 · DEPTH RAILS — MoGe for the near field, DA3 for the far field. One AtlasDepthMap per band, NOT one global default
3 · 🎯 SAM3 MATTES — sky · castle+rocks · water. Join terms with AND: a COMMA-separated prompt silently returns an EMPTY mask (measured 0.0%)
4 · ☁ SKY DOME + LaMa INPAINT — the classic DMP sky separation. ✂ crop spends LaMa's fixed 256² on the hole, not the whole 4K frame
5 · 📏 BOUNDED BAND — measures the castle's OWN depth extent and emits ONE cutoff that drives BOTH layers
6 · 🌊 WATER — band_geometry=ground: the ocean is analytically the Y=0 plane, so don't let depth noise make it lumpy
7 · 🏰 CASTLE + ROCKS — relief, clipped at the band cutoff, scoped to the SAM3 matte
8 · 🖼 MASTER DMP VIEWPORT — sky + water + castle. 📽 Project is the product; grey mesh is the diagnostic
9 · 🔧 EXPORT MESH + HOLE FILL + PREVIEW — a SEPARATE branch off the gated solve: clean-plate layers live in projection_sources, but the exporter reads proxy_geometry
